[STATUS] Accepted
[DATE] 2026-05-02

[DECISION] Pro tier adds WebSocket as a second NCP transport alongside HTTP/NWP.
[DECISION] HTTP/NWP remains the default; WebSocket NCP is opt-in via --ncp-mode websocket.
[DECISION] Both transports share the same BanyanMemoryProvider backend and NID auth logic.
[DECISION] Tier2 MsgPack encoding (NPS.Core.Codecs.Tier2MsgPackCodec) is unlocked on the WebSocket path.

[RATIONALE] NWP is already "NCP over HTTP" (Tier1 JSON) — WebSocket only changes the transport, not the frame model.
[RATIONALE] ASP.NET Core MapWebSocket lets HTTP and WS coexist on the same port and Kestrel listener with no infra change.
[RATIONALE] Full-duplex WebSocket enables server-push (e.g. memory-change notifications to subscribed agents) which HTTP polling cannot do efficiently.
[RATIONALE] NID auth reuses NidAuthenticationMiddleware: first WS frame carries the IdentFrame instead of an HTTP header.
[RATIONALE] Firewall-friendly: same port 17435 as HTTP, no new firewall rules needed.

[IMPACT] New file: Banyan.Web/Transport/WebSocketNcpHandler.cs — WS upgrade → frame loop (decode QueryFrame → MemoryProvider → encode response → send).
[IMPACT] New file: Banyan.Web/Transport/NcpFrameCodec.cs — thin wrapper over NPS.Core.Codecs selecting Tier1/Tier2 based on client negotiation header.
[IMPACT] WebApp.cs: app.MapWebSocket("/ncp", handler) registered when --ncp-mode websocket is set.
[IMPACT] WebOptions.cs: NcpMode enum { None, WebSocket } + --ncp-mode CLI flag.
[IMPACT] Agents using HTTP are unaffected — the /api/memory/* routes stay unchanged.
[IMPACT] Pro deployment guide must document WebSocket connection flow and Tier2 negotiation header.

[CONTEXT] Tier2 MsgPack is available in NPS.Core but unused in Lite (everything is Tier1 JSON).
[CONTEXT] The WebSocket path is internal to Pro nodes; external agent SDK still speaks HTTP in the common case.
[CONTEXT] Raw TCP NCP (Ent) supersedes WebSocket for intra-cluster Anchor→Memory traffic — see ADR-002.
